title: HMI external tag binding — symbolic vs absolute (reference only)
description: Human-readable examples. Prefer symbolic when PLC compiles and connection uses correct S7-1200/1500 driver. Absolute strings must be copied from TIA for optimized DBs.
absoluteFallbackNotes: Only when symbolic stays red after connection/driver/subnet checks.
For optimized DB, do NOT hand-guess byte offsets; use TIA 'Copy address' or disable optimized access on a dedicated HMI shadow DB.
Example shape (non-optimized DB200, first Bool member — illustrative only): %DB200.DBX0.0
